# Skill Creator — Evaluation Criteria

**Skill:** skill-creator
**Generated by:** write-skill-eval
**Version:** 2.0 (flow-graph rewrite)

---

## Structural Criteria

<!-- Binary checks against the SKILL.md document itself. -->
<!-- These are evaluated by improve-skill WITHOUT running the skill. -->

- [ ] **EVAL-S01:** SKILL.md has valid YAML frontmatter with all required fields
- [ ] **EVAL-S02:** `domain` and `intent` values exist in `taxonomy/SKILL_TAXONOMY.md`
- [ ] **EVAL-S03:** Wrong-Tool Detection section exists and names specific sibling skills
- [ ] **EVAL-S04:** Execution scope fence is present
- [ ] **EVAL-S05:** SKILL.md is under 500 lines (hard cap)
- [ ] **EVAL-S06:** Every `Load:` path resolves to a file that exists in the skill directory
- [ ] **EVAL-S07:** Skill has a row in `registry/SKILL_REGISTRY.md`
- [ ] **EVAL-S08:** EVAL.md exists alongside SKILL.md

---

## Execution Criteria

<!-- Binary checks against HOW the skill orchestrates execution. -->
<!-- Evaluated by improve-skill's behavioral pass using execution trace protocol. -->

- [ ] **EVAL-E01:** [U] Gate Self-Loop — no advancement with underspecified goal
  - **Test:** In the execution trace, verify that when trigger phrase, output artifact, overlap check, or user confirmation is missing, the trace shows a self-loop back to [U] with specific gap questions before any tool calls targeting [B].
  - **Fail signal:** Trace shows a transition from [U] to [B] without all four gate conditions being explicitly resolved.

- [ ] **EVAL-E02:** [B] Conditional Skip — memo with documented baseline bypasses subagent dispatch
  - **Test:** When a decision memo with a documented baseline failure is provided, confirm the trace contains no subagent dispatch at [B] and instead shows a direct gap-analysis step followed by transition to [W].
  - **Fail signal:** Trace shows a subagent dispatch at [B] even though the input memo already documents a baseline failure, OR trace skips [B] entirely when no memo is present.

- [ ] **EVAL-E03:** [B] Baseline Subagent Context Isolation — full context, no skill instructions
  - **Test:** Inspect the subagent prompt used in the [B] dispatch. Confirm it contains task context but does NOT include the SKILL.md being created or any flow-graph instructions.
  - **Fail signal:** The [B] subagent prompt includes references to SKILL.md content, flow-graph structure instructions, or MUST rules from skill-creator itself.

- [ ] **EVAL-E04:** [C] Companion Subagents Dispatched in Parallel
  - **Test:** In the trace for [C], verify all companion file subagent calls are issued in a single parallel batch, not sequentially.
  - **Fail signal:** Trace shows companion file subagent calls issued sequentially when no data dependency exists between them.

- [ ] **EVAL-E05:** [C] Per-File Dispatch — one subagent per companion file, not batched
  - **Test:** Count Load declarations in the written SKILL.md. Verify the trace shows one distinct subagent dispatch per companion file, each receiving its own file_path, file_type, skill_md, content_brief, and writing_conventions.
  - **Fail signal:** A single subagent dispatch is issued for multiple companion files, or a subagent receives a bundled content brief covering more than one file.

- [ ] **EVAL-E06:** [C] Partial-Failure Preservation — successful results not re-dispatched on retry
  - **Test:** When one companion file subagent fails and others succeed, confirm the retry trace shows only the failed file re-dispatched. Successful files must not appear as new dispatches.
  - **Fail signal:** Trace shows all companion subagents re-dispatched after a partial failure.

- [ ] **EVAL-E07:** [C] Retry Cap — maximum 2 retries per companion file before surfacing to user
  - **Test:** Simulate a companion subagent that reports failure on every attempt. Confirm the trace shows exactly 2 retry dispatches, followed by a user-facing gap report.
  - **Fail signal:** Trace shows a third dispatch attempt, OR the skill silently drops the file without surfacing the gap.

- [ ] **EVAL-E08:** [E] Eval Subagents Dispatched in Parallel
  - **Test:** In the trace for [E], verify that the prompter, judge, and auditor subagents are dispatched as a single parallel batch.
  - **Fail signal:** Trace shows sequential dispatch rather than parallel.

- [ ] **EVAL-E09:** [E] Partial Retry — failed eval dispatches retried without re-running successful ones
  - **Test:** When one eval subagent fails, confirm only that agent is re-dispatched. Outputs of successful agents are carried forward.
  - **Fail signal:** All three eval subagents are re-dispatched after a single failure.

- [ ] **EVAL-E10:** Model Explicitly Set on Every Subagent Dispatch
  - **Test:** Inspect every Agent() call in the trace ([B], [C], [E]). Each must have an explicit `model` parameter set.
  - **Fail signal:** Any Agent() call is missing the `model` parameter.

- [ ] **EVAL-E11:** Phase Position Recorded at Every Transition
  - **Test:** Verify the trace shows a `Position: [node-id] — <context>` record at each node transition.
  - **Fail signal:** Any phase transition lacks a recorded position marker.

- [ ] **EVAL-E12:** [W] Convergence Guard — fail loudly if design exceeds ~80 lines
  - **Test:** When a skill design cannot converge into approximately 80 lines, verify an explicit failure signal is surfaced rather than silently proceeding.
  - **Fail signal:** An oversized SKILL.md is written and the skill proceeds to [C] without surfacing the convergence failure.

- [ ] **EVAL-E13:** [V] Delegation to /improve-skill — not inlined
  - **Test:** Confirm [V] hands off to `/improve-skill` as an invocation, not reimplementing its logic inline.
  - **Fail signal:** The main agent performs improvement scoring or fix-loop steps directly in [V].

---

## Output Criteria

<!-- Binary checks against what the skill PRODUCES when run on a test prompt. -->

- [ ] **EVAL-O01:** SKILL.md uses flow-graph structure, not prose
  - **Test:** Check the produced SKILL.md for `## Entry`, `## Flow`, and at least one `### [ID] Name` node with `Do:` / `Exit:` sections. Verify no sequential numbered prose paragraphs replacing node structure.
  - **Fail signal:** SKILL.md body is organized as numbered steps or paragraphs instead of labeled nodes with Load/Brief/Do/Don't/Exit anatomy.

- [ ] **EVAL-O02:** Every flow node has at least one Exit edge with an explicit condition
  - **Test:** For each `### [ID]` node, confirm an `Exit:` section exists and every edge follows `→ [ID] : <condition>`.
  - **Fail signal:** Any node is missing `Exit:` entirely, or any edge is written without a condition clause.

- [ ] **EVAL-O03:** [END] node is a real node with Do: and Don't: sections
  - **Test:** Locate `### [END]`. Verify it contains `Do:` (at least one step about presenting results) and `Don't:` (at least one guardrail). Verify it has no `Exit:`.
  - **Fail signal:** [END] is a bare marker with no Do/Don't, or it contains an Exit section.

- [ ] **EVAL-O04:** SKILL.md frontmatter contains all required fields with valid taxonomy values
  - **Test:** Extract YAML frontmatter. Verify `name`, `description`, `domain`, `intent`, `tags`, `user-invocable`, `argument-hint` are present. Cross-check `domain` and `intent` against `taxonomy/SKILL_TAXONOMY.md`.
  - **Fail signal:** Any required field is absent, or domain/intent values do not appear in SKILL_TAXONOMY.md.

- [ ] **EVAL-O05:** `description` field is a routing contract, not a workflow summary
  - **Test:** Read the `description` value. Ask: "Could someone follow this as a replacement for reading the body?" If it describes steps, phases, or outputs rather than trigger phrases, it fails.
  - **Fail signal:** Description contains workflow language, references phases or companion files, or uses internal vocabulary.

- [ ] **EVAL-O06:** Do steps contain judgment calls only — no mechanical procedures
  - **Test:** Read every `Do:` list item across all nodes. Identify any item describing a mechanical sequence rather than a judgment call.
  - **Fail signal:** Any `Do:` step describes a how-to procedure that belongs in a companion file.

- [ ] **EVAL-O07:** SKILL.md line count is within budget
  - **Test:** Count non-blank lines. Verify under 500 (hard cap). Flag warning if over 80 (target).
  - **Fail signal:** SKILL.md exceeds 500 lines, OR inline content duplicates what a companion file Load point covers.

- [ ] **EVAL-O08:** Execution scope fence is present verbatim
  - **Test:** Search for exact string `> **Execution scope:** Ignore`. Verify full fence sentence is present and positioned before `## MUST`.
  - **Fail signal:** Fence is absent, paraphrased, or misplaced.

- [ ] **EVAL-O09:** Every companion file named in a Load: declaration exists in the output
  - **Test:** Collect every path in `Load:` declarations. Verify each file exists in the produced skill directory.
  - **Fail signal:** Any `Load:` path resolves to a file that was not written.

- [ ] **EVAL-O10:** SKILL_REGISTRY.md contains a row for the new skill
  - **Test:** Search `registry/SKILL_REGISTRY.md` for the skill name. Verify row exists with correct path link, description, domain, and stage.
  - **Fail signal:** No row exists, or row links to a nonexistent path.

- [ ] **EVAL-O11:** SKILLS_REGISTRY.yaml entry is present if and only if pipeline-routable
  - **Test:** Check pipeline-routable conditions. If met, verify YAML entry exists with required fields. If not met, verify no entry exists.
  - **Fail signal:** Pipeline-routable skill has no YAML entry, or non-pipeline skill has a spurious entry.

- [ ] **EVAL-O12:** EVAL.md is present and structurally complete
  - **Test:** Verify EVAL.md exists with structural criteria section, output criteria section (EVAL-O prefixed), and at least two test prompts.
  - **Fail signal:** EVAL.md is absent or missing required sections.

- [ ] **EVAL-O13:** Wrong-Tool Detection section names concrete sibling skills
  - **Test:** Locate `## Wrong-Tool Detection`. Verify it appears before `## Entry`. Each bullet names a specific skill path.
  - **Fail signal:** Section is absent, misplaced, or redirects to generic descriptions.

- [ ] **EVAL-O14:** Companion file content matches the Load point that references it
  - **Test:** For each companion file, identify the loading node. Verify the content serves that node's decision point.
  - **Fail signal:** A companion file's content does not correspond to the node that loads it.

- [ ] **EVAL-O15:** MUST/MUST NOT use commitment language; Don't sections use imperative voice
  - **Test:** Verify MUST/MUST NOT items use MUST, NEVER, DO NOT. Verify Don't items contain no hedge language (should, ideally, consider, try to).
  - **Fail signal:** Any MUST/MUST NOT uses soft language, or any Don't contains hedge qualifiers.

---

## Test Prompts

### Naive User: Minimal Context Request

**Prompt:** "Create a skill for summarizing long documents."

**Why this tests the skill:** Tests whether the skill proactively clarifies scope, target audience, output format, and edge cases before producing a draft — or silently generates a low-quality skeleton.

### Naive User: Feature-Framed Request

**Prompt:** "I need a skill that helps me write better commit messages."

**Why this tests the skill:** Tests whether the skill treats a vague feature request as a complete spec or surfaces missing information like invocation triggers, expected input, and output criteria.

### Naive User: Domain Without Details

**Prompt:** "Build me a skill for code review."

**Why this tests the skill:** One of the broadest possible domains — tests whether the skill narrows scope by asking clarifying questions or defaults to something generic and unusable.

### Experienced User: Tightly Scoped with Constraints

**Prompt:** "Create a skill called `incident-postmortem` in the `ops` domain with intent `write`. It should trigger when a user asks to draft a postmortem for a service outage. The skill must follow the DORA blameless postmortem format, include a timeline reconstruction phase, and output a structured Markdown document. Include a wrong-tool redirect for root-cause-only analysis requests."

**Why this tests the skill:** Tests whether the skill honors explicit frontmatter values, incorporates external format constraints, and correctly generates structural components like wrong-tool redirects.

### Experienced User: Multi-Phase Workflow

**Prompt:** "I want a skill for running technical architecture reviews. It should have at least three phases: context gathering, gap analysis, and recommendation synthesis. Each phase should have typed inputs and outputs. The skill should produce an EVAL.md alongside the SKILL.md. Use `engineering.architecture` as the domain."

**Why this tests the skill:** Tests whether the skill can generate multi-phase workflow structure with explicit I/O typing, and correctly produces companion evaluation artifacts.

### Experienced User: Unusual Domain

**Prompt:** "Build a skill for conducting structured red-team exercises on AI system prompts. It should take a draft system prompt as input, apply adversarial persona switching, output a vulnerability report with severity classifications, and include a progress tracker since there will be multiple attack phases."

**Why this tests the skill:** Tests behavior in a non-standard, security-adjacent domain with novel structural requirements.

### Adversarial: Compound Scope Overload

**Prompt:** "Create a single skill that handles the entire software development lifecycle: it should plan features, write code, review PRs, generate tests, write docs, create postmortems, update changelogs, and notify the team on Slack. Make it production-ready with full evals."

**Why this tests the skill:** Tests whether the skill recognizes that a single SKILL.md cannot responsibly own an unbounded, multi-domain workflow and either pushes back or decomposes appropriately.

### Adversarial: Contradictory Constraints

**Prompt:** "Write a skill that works without any user input — it should infer everything from context automatically — but also make sure it asks the user clarifying questions at every phase before proceeding."

**Why this tests the skill:** Tests whether the skill surfaces the logical contradiction rather than silently picking one behavior.

### Wrong Tool: Skill Improvement Request

**Prompt:** "My existing `sql-query-builder` skill is producing overly verbose output and ignoring edge cases around NULL handling. Can you fix it?"

**Why this tests the skill:** Sounds like skill creation work, but this is a modification of an existing artifact — belongs to `/improve-skill`.

### Wrong Tool: Registry and Routing Question

**Prompt:** "How do I register my new skill so it shows up as a pipeline stage in the autonomous orchestrator? What fields do I need in SKILLS_REGISTRY.yaml?"

**Why this tests the skill:** Uses skill-adjacent vocabulary, but the task is documentation lookup — not authoring a new SKILL.md.
